U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) has released footage showing precision strikes on Iranian loitering munitions prior to launch, including an Iranian Army Arash-2 and IRGC Shahed-136 drones, carried out by a U.S. Army MQ-9A Reaper. The Arash-2 was targeted with a 500-pound precision-guided bomb (likely a GBU-38, GBU-54, or GBU-12), while the Shahed drones were struck using AGM-114 Hellfire or AGM-179 Joint Air-to-Ground Missile (JAGM) missiles. The footage also shows the use of low-cost single-unit launchers, with the drones deployed using solid-fuel JATO boosters.

Hezbollah has released footage of a missile attack targeting the headquarters of the Israeli Navy’s Shayetet 13 naval commando unit at the Atlit Naval Base, south of Haifa, using Fath-360 (BM-120) short-range ballistic missiles (SRBMs).
The Israeli Air Force (IAF) carried out airstrikes on targets in Tehran, which may have included energy infrastructure.
Footage shows an Iranian ballistic missile striking central Israel during the latest wave of attacks.

The Financial Times reports that the strikes on QatarEnergy’s Ras Laffan Industrial City involved advanced, maneuverable missiles capable of evading U.S.-made MIM-104 Patriot defenses, according to an official briefed on the attack. There is growing speculation that the Fattah-2 hypersonic glide vehicle (HGV) was used for the first time in the conflict, though I doubt that. There is little strategic logic in expending an expensive HGV on a relatively accessible target.
